notes: Functional end-to-end dogfood (not baseline-vs-skill): without the skill the agent wouldn't call Codex, vet, or persist state, so there is no sensible 'no-skill' baseline. These five target DISTINCT critical paths with no overlap: (1) iterable round 1, (2) multi-round continuation, (3) prompt-only consult, (4) code change-spec, (5) privacy-mode. Triggering and effort-parsing are folded as expectations inside evals 1/3/4; context-resolution is folded into eval 2 (it resolves the in-progress spec from the conversation). Evals are LIVE runs needing an authenticated Codex CLI (they cost money and edit files), so they are a manual pre-release gate, not CI; CI only checks this file exists, parses, and is non-empty. Setup: eval 1 needs a short throwaway spec at the referenced path; eval 2 must run IMMEDIATELY AFTER eval 1 in the same conversation/workdir (it depends on eval 1's spec + state); evals 4-5 each need a tiny throwaway code file at the referenced path.

evals.prompt: I've just finished the spec for an internal URL-shortener service (/tmp/spec-debate-test/url-shortener-spec.md). Run it through a debate with codex and improve it.
evals.expected_output: Round 1: Claude and codex each independently propose improvements (the Codex prompt does NOT contain Claude's current-round proposal list); Claude merges both lists with veto (verifying against the actual file, not blindly), applies accepted ones via Edit, writes a skimmable report (accepted/partial/rejected with one-line reasons and source codex|own), and creates .url-shortener-spec.md.debate-state.json with round 1 recorded. Effort defaults to high (no effort word in the prompt).
expectations: A Codex critique ran via scripts/run_codex_critique.sh (exactly one codex exec at a time)
The Codex prompt file did NOT contain Claude's own current-round proposal list (independence preserved)
The report and state include `own`-sourced findings that went through the same accept/partial/reject vetting as Codex's (rejected own proposals are reported whenever they occur)
Every finding has a one-line reason; effort defaulted to high (no effort directive in the prompt)
Accepted/partial edits were applied to the spec file via Edit, in the spec's voice
.url-shortener-spec.md.debate-state.json was created beside the spec and parses as a single valid JSON document with round 1 recorded

evals.prompt: Do one more iteration of improvements on the spec.
evals.expected_output: Run immediately after eval 1 in the same conversation. The skill resolves 'the spec' from conversation context (no path given), reads the existing .url-shortener-spec.md.debate-state.json, sets round=2, passes settled (rejected/partial) verdicts to codex so they are not re-raised, gathers fresh proposals from both models, merges with veto, applies accepted edits, reports with convergence status, and appends round 2 to the state file.
expectations: The spec was resolved from the conversation context (the prompt gives no path), not guessed across the filesystem
The existing debate-state file was loaded and the new round numbered 2
Settled rejected/partial verdicts from round 1 were passed to Codex as not-to-re-raise
Fresh proposals were gathered from both models and merged with veto
The report includes a convergence assessment, and round 2 was appended with the state still parsing as one valid JSON document

evals.prompt: I'm torn between Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY and a small Redis stream for the job queue in our internal tool (~5 jobs/sec, one Postgres already in the stack). Get codex's take on which fits better.
evals.expected_output: The skill triggers on the explicit second-opinion request even though the words 'debate' and 'spec' do not appear. It picks the prompt-only path (a bounded advisory question), runs exactly one Codex pass via the helper, and reports inline: Codex's position, Claude's own vetted take (Step 4 lens), the prompt-file path, and a one-line 'sent to Codex' note. It creates no debate-state file and runs no rounds (offering to escalate only if the question turns out under-scoped mid-pass).
expectations: Triggered on a casual second-opinion request with neither 'debate' nor 'spec' in the prompt
Explicitly chose and stated prompt-only mode
Exactly one codex pass via scripts/run_codex_critique.sh; no rounds were run
No new subject-specific .debate-state.json file was created for this question
The inline report gives Codex's position, Claude's own vetted take, the prompt-file path, and a one-line 'sent to Codex' note

evals.prompt: think it through with codex at maximum reasoning depth before I refactor the rate-limiter in /tmp/spec-debate-test4/ratelimit.py — don't touch the code yet.
evals.expected_output: The skill triggers (no 'debate'/'spec' wording), parses 'maximum reasoning depth' as xhigh, and recognizes CODE mode: it drafts a change-spec to a file from the task + the code, runs one debate round at xhigh via the helper, and merges with veto. The source code is NOT edited during the debate; the change-spec carries acceptance checks/tests and treats applying-to-code as a separate post-debate step.
expectations: Triggered on 'think it through with codex' (no 'debate'/'spec' wording)
'maximum reasoning depth' was parsed as effort xhigh and passed to the helper
CODE mode recognized: a change-spec file was drafted and the source code (ratelimit.py) was NOT edited during the debate
A debate round completed with the merge-with-veto report
The change-spec includes acceptance checks/tests and treats applying-to-code as a separate post-debate step

evals.prompt: Debate this billing module's redesign with codex, but privacy mode — don't send the actual code, just an abstracted summary. The goal is to make invoice creation idempotent so webhook retries can't double-charge. Code is in /tmp/spec-debate-test5/billing.py.
evals.expected_output: The skill triggers and honors privacy-mode: Codex receives only an approved abstracted summary built around the stated goal (idempotent invoice creation / no double-charge on webhook retries), never the raw code; the codex prompt file contains no verbatim source or secrets. Claude marks Codex's confidence as limited (it judged from a summary) and still produces a vetted report — or declines the Codex pass with a reason if the question cannot be judged without the code.
expectations: Privacy-mode was recognized from 'privacy mode / don't send the code'
The codex prompt file contained only an abstracted summary (built around the idempotency goal) — no verbatim source code from billing.py
Codex's confidence was marked as limited (judged from a summary, not the code)
A vetted report was produced, or the Codex pass was declined with a clear reason if unjudgeable from the summary
